samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="IDLE">0</constant>
        <constant id="SHIFT_ACCUMULATE">1</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <signal id="i" type="reg" width_description="[4:0]">Shift count register initialized to 0.</signal>
        <signal id="done_r" type="reg">Multiplication completion flag initialized to 0.</signal>
        <signal id="areg" type="reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to store the multiplicand (a).</signal>
        <signal id="breg" type="reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to store the multiplier (b).</signal>
        <signal id="yout_r" type="reg" width_description="[31:0]">Register to store the product output (yout).</signal>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset the shift count register to 0
                i <= 0;
            end else if (start && i < 17) begin
                // Increment the shift count register by 1
                i <= i + 1;
            end else begin
                // Reset the shift count register to 0
                i <= 0;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Set the multiplication completion flag to 0
                done_r <= 0;
            end else if (i == 16) begin
                // Set the multiplication completion flag to 1
                done_r <= 1;
            end else if (i == 17) begin
                // Reset the multiplication completion flag to 0
                done_r <= 0;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Initialize the multiplicand register to 0
                areg <= 0;
                // Initialize the multiplier register to 0
                breg <= 0;
                // Initialize the product output register to 0
                yout_r <= 0;
            end else if (start) begin
                // Load the multiplicand into the multiplicand register
                areg <= ain;
                // Load the multiplier into the multiplier register
                breg <= bin;
                
                // Perform shift and accumulate operation based on the shift count register
                case(i)
                    0: yout_r <= {16'd0, areg};
                    default: yout_r <= yout_r + ({16'd0, breg} << (i - 1));
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is active, initialize the shift count register to 0. Otherwise, increment the shift count register by 1 if the start signal is active and the shift count register is less than 17. Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is active, initialize the multiplication completion flag to 0. Otherwise, set the multiplication completion flag to 1 if the shift count register is equal to 16, and set the multiplication completion flag to 0 if the shift count register is equal to 17. Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is active, initialize the multiplicand register to 0, the multiplier register to 0, and the product output register to 0. Otherwise, load the multiplicand into the multiplicand register, load the multiplier into the multiplier register, and perform shift and accumulate operation based on the shift count register.</implementation>
</module>
